contentBlocks.original:
उद्यन्नद्यविवस्वानारोहन्नुत्तरां दिवं देवः ।
हृद्रोगं मम सूर्यो हरिमाणं चाऽऽशु नाशयतु ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
udyannadyavivasvānārōhannuttarāṃ divaṃ dēvaḥ ।
hṛdrōgaṃ mama sūryō harimāṇaṃ chā''śu nāśayatu ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The Sun (Vivasvān), rising today and ascending higher in the northern sky — may that Sūrya swiftly destroy the heart-disease (hṛd-roga) and the jaundice (harimāṇa) within me.
contentBlocks.meanings: udyan: rising | adya: today | vivasvān: the Sun (Vivasvān) | ārohann: ascending | uttarāṃ divam: higher into the northern sky | deva: the divine one | hṛd-rogam: heart-disease | mama: my/within me | sūryo: the Sun | harimāṇam ca: and jaundice | āśu: swiftly | nāśayatu: may he destroy

contentBlocks.original:
निमिषार्धेनैकेन द्वे च शते द्वे सहस्रे द्वे ।
क्रममाण योजनानां नमोऽस्तु ते नलिननाथाय ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nimiṣārdhēnaikēna dvē cha śatē dvē sahasrē dvē ।
kramamāṇa yōjanānāṃ namō'stu tē naḻinanāthāya ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Traversing two hundred, two thousand, and two [more] thousand yojanas (units of distance) in half the blink of an eye — salutations to you, O lord of the lotus (Nalina-nātha, the Sun who opens lotuses).
contentBlocks.meanings: nimiṣārdha: half the blink of an eye | ekena: in one (such unit of time) | dve ca śate: two hundred | dve sahasre: two thousand | dve: and two [more] | kramamāṇa: traversing | yojanānām: of yojanas (units of distance) | namo'stu te: salutations to you | nalina-nāthāya: to the lord of the lotus (the Sun)

contentBlocks.original:
कर्मज्ञानखदशकं मनश्च जीव इति विश्वसर्गाय ।
द्वादशधा यो विचरति स द्वादशमूर्तिरस्तु मोदाय ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
karmajñānakhadaśakaṃ manaścha jīva iti viśvasargāya ।
dvādaśadhā yō vicharati sa dvādaśamūrtirastu mōdāya ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: He who moves in twelve forms — the five organs of action, five organs of knowledge, the mind, and the jīva (individual soul) — for the purpose of the world's creation — may that twelve-formed one (Dvādaśa-mūrti) bring us joy.
contentBlocks.meanings: karma-jñāna-kha-daśakaṃ: the ten of action and knowledge organs | manaś ca: and the mind | jīva: the individual soul | iti: thus | viśva-sargāya: for the creation of the world | dvādaśadhā: in twelve forms | yo vicarati: who moves/operates | dvādaśa-mūrtiḥ: the twelve-formed one | astu modāya: may he bring joy

contentBlocks.original:
त्वं हि यजूऋक्सामः त्वमागमस्त्वं वषट्कारः ।
त्वं विश्वं त्वं हंसः त्वं भानो परमहंसश्च ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tvaṃ hi yajūṛksāmaḥ tvamāgamastvaṃ vaṣaṭkāraḥ ।
tvaṃ viśvaṃ tvaṃ haṃsaḥ tvaṃ bhānō paramahaṃsaścha ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: You are the Yajur Veda, the Ṛg Veda, the Sāma Veda — you are the Āgama (Tantra scripture) — you are the vaṣaṭkāra (the ritual exclamation) — you are the universe, you are the haṃsa (the individual soul / the sacred bird) — O Sun! You are the Paramahahṃsa (the supreme soul) too.
contentBlocks.meanings: tvam hi: you indeed are | yajuḥ: the Yajur Veda | ṛk: the Ṛg Veda | sāmaḥ: the Sāma Veda | āgamaḥ: the Āgama scripture | vaṣaṭkāraḥ: the vaṣaṭ exclamation (ritual declaration at the moment of oblation) | viśvam: the universe | haṃsaḥ: the haṃsa (individual self / sacred goose) | bhāno: O Sun | paramahaṃsaḥ: the Paramahaṃsa (the Supreme Soul / a great renunciate)

contentBlocks.original:
शिवरूपात् ज्ञानमहं त्वत्तो मुक्तिं जनार्दनाकारात् ।
शिखिरूपादैश्वर्यं त्वत्तश्चारोग्यमिच्छामि ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śivarūpāt jñānamahaṃ tvattō muktiṃ janārdanākārāt ।
śikhirūpādaiśvaryaṃ tvattaśchārōgyamichChāmi ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Sun — from your Śiva-form (the auspicious, fiery form) I seek knowledge; from your Janārdana-form (the Viṣṇu-form) I seek liberation; from your Agni-form (fire aspect) I seek sovereignty; and from you I seek health.
contentBlocks.meanings: śiva-rūpāt: from your Śiva-form | jñānam: knowledge | tvattas: from you | muktim: liberation | janārdana-ākārāt: from your Janārdana (Viṣṇu) form | śikhi-rūpāt: from your Agni (fire) form | aiśvaryam: sovereignty | tvattas ca: and from you | ārogyam: health | icchāmi: I seek

contentBlocks.original:
त्वचि दोषा दृशि दोषाः हृदि दोषा येऽखिलेन्द्रियजदोषाः ।
तान् पूषा हतदोषः किञ्चिद्रोषाग्निना दहतु ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tvachi dōṣā dṛśi dōṣāḥ hṛdi dōṣā yē'khilēndriyajadōṣāḥ ।
tān pūṣā hatadōṣaḥ kiñchidrōṣāgninā dahatu ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The defects of the skin, the defects of the eyes, the defects of the heart, and all defects arising from every sense organ — may Pūṣan (the Sun as nourisher), who has destroyed all defects, burn them away with a small fire of anger.
contentBlocks.meanings: tvaci doṣā: defects of the skin | dṛśi doṣā: defects of the eyes | hṛdi doṣā: defects of the heart | ye'khila-indriya-ja-doṣā: all defects arising from sense organs | pūṣā: Pūṣan (the nourishing Sun) | hata-doṣā: who has destroyed all defects | kiñcid-roṣa-agninā: with a small fire of anger | dahatu: may he burn

contentBlocks.translation: O fierce-rayed Sun (Caṇḍāṃśu) — may you shatter the diseases that obstruct the four aims of life (dharma, artha, kāma, mokṣa), that cause intense affliction, and that have imprisoned and bound the entire company of senses.
contentBlocks.meanings: dharma-artha-kāma-mokṣa-pratirodhāna: blocking/obstructing the four aims of life | ugra-tāpa-vega-karān: causing intense affliction and pain | bandī-kṛta-indriya-gaṇān: having imprisoned the entire company of senses | gadān: diseases | vikhaṇḍayatu: may he shatter/break | caṇḍāṃśuḥ: the fierce-rayed Sun

contentBlocks.original:
येन विनेदं तिमिरं जगदेत्य ग्रसति चरमचरमखिलम् ।
धृतबोधं तं नलिनीभर्तारं हर्तारमापदामीडे ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yēna vinēdaṃ timiraṃ jagadētya grasati charamacharamakhilam ।
dhṛtabōdhaṃ taṃ naḻinībhartāraṃ hartāramāpadāmīḍē ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Without whom this darkness seizes the entire world — moving and unmoving — I praise that lord of the lotus (Nalina-bhartā), the holder of light (dhṛta-bodha), the destroyer of adversities.
contentBlocks.meanings: yena vinā: without whom | idam timiraṃ: this darkness | jagat etya: having come to the world | graṣati: seizes | cara-acara-akhilam: all moving and unmoving things | dhṛta-bodha: holder of light/consciousness | nalina-bhartāraṃ: lord of the lotus | hartāram āpadām: destroyer of adversities | īḍe: I praise

contentBlocks.original:
यस्य सहस्राभीशोरभीशु लेशो हिमांशुबिम्बगतः ।
भासयति नक्तमखिलं भेदयतु विपद्गणानरुणः ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yasya sahasrābhīśōrabhīśu lēśō himāṃśubimbagataḥ ।
bhāsayati naktamakhilaṃ bhēdayatu vipadgaṇānaruṇaḥ ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Even a fraction (leśa) of the rays of him who has a thousand rays — entering the disc of the cool-rayed moon — illuminates the entire night. May Aruṇa (the Sun's charioteer / the dawn) shatter our multitudes of adversities.
contentBlocks.meanings: yasya sahasra-abhīśoḥ: of him who has a thousand rays | abhīśu-leśaḥ: a fraction of a ray | himāṃśu-bimba-gataḥ: entering the disc of the cool-rayed moon | bhāsayati: illuminates | naktam: the night | akhilam: entirely | bhedayatu: may he shatter | vipad-gaṇān: multitudes of adversities | aruṇaḥ: Aruṇa (dawn / the Sun's red charioteer)

contentBlocks.original:
तिमिरमिव नेत्रतिमिरं पटलमिवाऽशेषरोगपटलं नः ।
काशमिवाधिनिकायं कालपिता रोगयुक्ततां हरतात् ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
timiramiva nētratimiraṃ paṭalamivā'śēṣarōgapaṭalaṃ naḥ ।
kāśamivādhinikāyaṃ kālapitā rōgayuktatāṃ haratāt ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: As the Sun disperses eye-darkness (timir), may Kāla's father (the Sun, who is Kāla's father — or, Time's father, i.e., the Sun who governs time) dispel for us the mass of all diseases like a veil — and the entire aggregation of mental afflictions like darkness.
contentBlocks.meanings: timiram iva: like eye-darkness/cataract | netra-timiraṃ: darkness/disease of the eyes | paṭalam iva: like a veil/curtain | aśeṣa-roga-paṭalam naḥ: for us the mass of all diseases | kāśam iva: like ākāśa/sky | adhi-nikāyam: the aggregation of mental afflictions | kāla-pitā: father of Kāla (the Sun, who governs Time) | roga-yuktatāṃ harathāt: may he remove the condition of disease

contentBlocks.original:
वाताश्मरीगदार्शस्त्वग्दोषमहोदरप्रमेहांश्च ।
ग्रहणीभगन्धराख्या महतीस्त्वं मे रुजो हंसि ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: You destroy in me the diseases of wind (vāta), urinary calculi (aśmarī), piles (arśas), skin diseases (tvag-doṣa), abdominal diseases (mahodara), diabetes (prameha), conditions of the rectum (grahaṇī), and the great conditions named bhagandara (fistula) — O Sun.
contentBlocks.meanings: vāta: wind disease | aśmarī: kidney/urinary calculi | gada: disease | arśas: piles/hemorrhoids | tvag-doṣa: skin diseases | mahodara: abdominal/dropsy conditions | prameha: diabetes/urinary disorders | grahaṇī: rectum/digestive disorders | bhagandara: fistula (perianal condition) | ākhyā: named | mahatīs: great/serious | ruja: painful diseases | haṃsi: you destroy

contentBlocks.original:
त्वं माता त्वं शरणं त्वं धाता त्वं धनं त्वमाचार्यः ।
त्वं त्राता त्वं हर्ता विपदामर्क प्रसीद मम भानो ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tvaṃ mātā tvaṃ śaraṇaṃ tvaṃ dhātā tvaṃ dhanaṃ tvamāchāryaḥ ।
tvaṃ trātā tvaṃ hartā vipadāmarka prasīda mama bhānō ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: You are my mother, you are my refuge, you are the sustainer, you are my wealth, you are my teacher. You are my protector, you are the remover of adversities, O Sun — be gracious to me.
contentBlocks.meanings: tvam mātā: you are my mother | tvam śaraṇam: you are my refuge | tvam dhātā: you are the sustainer | tvam dhanam: you are my wealth | tvam ācāryaḥ: you are my teacher | tvam trātā: you are my protector | tvam hartā: you are the remover | vipadām: of adversities | arka: O Sun | prasīda me: be gracious to me

contentBlocks.original:
इत्यार्याद्वादशकं साम्बस्य पुरो नभःस्थलात्पतितम् ।
पठतां भाग्यसमृद्धिः समस्तरोगक्षयश्च स्यात् ॥ 13 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ityāryādvādaśakaṃ sāmbasya purō nabhaḥsthalātpatitam ।
paṭhatāṃ bhāgyasamṛddhiḥ samastarōgakṣayaścha syāt ॥ 13 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: This set of twelve āryā verses to the Sun — composed by Sāmba — fell from the sky region before him. For those who recite it, there will be enrichment of fortune and the removal of all diseases.
contentBlocks.meanings: iti āryā dvādaśakam: this set of twelve āryā verses | sāmbasya puraḥ: before Sāmba | nabhaḥ-sthala: the sky region | patitam: fell (from heaven) | paṭhatāṃ: for those who recite | bhāgya-samṛddhiḥ: enrichment of fortune | samasta-roga-kṣayaḥ ca: and the destruction of all diseases | syāt: shall be

contentBlocks.translation: Thus ends the Dvādaśa-Āryā Sūrya-Stuti composed by Sāmba.
contentBlocks.meanings: iti: thus ends | śrī sāmba-kṛta: composed by Sāmba | dvādaśa-āryā-sūrya-stutiḥ: the twelve Āryā-meter hymns to the Sun
